zh.lifeSummary: Hugh de Meynell是Mr Meignell和Philippa le Savage的兒子，於1274年出生在英國英格蘭德比夏爾柯克 明日香。他和Margaret De Meynell至少有1個女兒。他去世於1333年在英國英格蘭德比夏爾明日香 密爾享年59歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Meynell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 Norman, English: locative name from one of the French places named Mesnil, from Old French mesnil (Latin * mansionile, a diminutive of mansio ‘abode, habitation’), used of the country domain where a noble lived with his family and retainers. As a surname it may also be used of a member of the household, retainer. Some of the bearers listed below may belong to (2).
2 English: relationship name from the Continental Germanic female personal name Maginhild.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

en.lifeSummary: Hugh de Meynell was born in 1274, in Kirk Langley, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of Mr Meignell and Philippa le Savage. He had at least 1 daughter with Margaret De Meynell. He died in 1333, in Langley Mill, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 59.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh de Meynellは1274年にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアキルク ラングリーでMr MeignellとPhilippa le Savageの息子として生まれた。彼はMargaret De Meynellとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1333年にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアラングリー ミルで59歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh de Meynell(은)는 1274년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 커크 랭글리에서 Mr Meignell(와)과 Philippa le Savage의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Margaret De Meynell(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1333년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 랭글리 미에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Hugh de Meynell родился в 1274 г. в местности кирк лангли, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания. Его родителей звали Mr Meignell и Philippa le Savage. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Margaret De Meynell. Он умер в 1333 г. в местности лангли милл, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 59 лет.
